claim_boundary: Target registry only. These entries identify difficult public benchmarks and safe first-run paths; they are not leaderboard claims. Public comparison requires unchanged upstream harnesses, exact model/agent config, raw artifacts, and published receipts.
selection_rule: Prefer benchmarks where agent/tooling/verification can matter more than raw one-shot model text. Run blind first, then SCBE rescue/retry harness, then compare.

targets.why_hard: Official benchmark for terminal agents; recent paper/site reports frontier models and agents below 65 percent on Terminal-Bench 2.0.
targets.local_strategy: Use SCBE shell --agent-json as the agent adapter. Run a tiny task subset first, then blind full subset, then rescue-advisor retry.

targets.why_hard: Human-validated 500-instance subset of SWE-bench for real GitHub issue repair. Strong target for multi-step patch/test/retry systems.
targets.local_strategy: Start with one verified task in a clean worktree. Compare raw primary agent versus SCBE gated retry with advisor and test receipt.

targets.why_hard: Harder SWE-style variants and mutations reduce overfitting to known issue text. Use only after SWE-bench Verified adapter is stable.
targets.local_strategy: Do not claim public results until the upstream harness and split are exact. Use for internal blind stress tests.

targets.why_hard: Comprehensive chemistry LLM benchmark with chemistry and materials-science tasks; suitable for controlled advisor web/retrieval experiments.
targets.local_strategy: Start with a small deterministic text-only subset. Compare closed-book raw model, allowlisted web advisor, and verifier/retry where answers are checkable.

commands:
python - <<'PY'
from datasets import load_dataset
print(load_dataset('jablonkagroup/ChemBench', split='train[:5]'))
PY

targets.why_hard: Chemistry RAG benchmark for testing whether retrieval improves chemistry answers instead of just adding noise.
targets.local_strategy: Use SCBE controlled web advisor and local citation receipts. Score raw model versus retrieval-assisted answer with source constraints.

targets.why_hard: Expert-curated reasoning-intensive chemistry benchmark with multimodal and text-only formats. Harder to wire because multimodal handling is needed.
targets.local_strategy: Start text-only. Add image/multimodal only after the text scorer and provenance receipts are stable.

targets.why_hard: Molecular property prediction on QM9-style data tests data handling, feature engineering, model training, and reproducible scoring rather than chat-only chemistry.
targets.local_strategy: Use Kaggle API to fetch a small slice. Build a baseline descriptor model, then let SCBE generate/retry feature pipelines under a fixed metric.

targets.why_hard: Standard molecular property prediction tasks can test whether SCBE helps build correct ML pipelines, not just answer chemistry trivia.
targets.local_strategy: Start with a tiny split and a deterministic metric. Add graph-model or RDKit features only when dependencies are available.

commands:
python - <<'PY'
import importlib.util
print('rdkit', bool(importlib.util.find_spec('rdkit')))
print('torch', bool(importlib.util.find_spec('torch')))
PY
